summary: This cluster investigates the biosynthesis of mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) in cyanobacteria, specifically focusing on the species *Sphaerospermopsis torques-reginae* ITEP-024. Research demonstrated that this organism produces the MGA compound (mycosporine-glycine-alanine) through a novel metabolic pathway that utilizes sedoheptulose 7-phosphate from the pentose phosphate pathway, with evidence also supporting the involvement of the shikimic acid pathway. A key finding across the cluster was that MAA production is significantly enhanced under UV radiation exposure, indicating a photoprotective ecological function. The geographic and ecological scope centers on freshwater or terrestrial cyanobacterial strains, with the work providing genetic and biochemical evidence for redundant biosynthetic routes within a single species.
